name: advance-meta
title: Advance Meta
purpose: Capability: Cross-cutting ADV concerns — config diagnostics, metadata filters, shutdown lifecycle, due-diligence routing, and synthetic-state guards. Split from `advance` capability.

requirements.title: Synthetic Validation Draft Isolation
requirements.body: Supported internal validation or parity flows must not leave synthetic draft changes in live ADV project state. Protection must preserve legitimate user-created drafts and keep draft/status surfaces focused on real changes.

scenarios.title: Synthetic validation families blocked on supported create path
given: A supported internal validation or parity flow attempts to create a synthetic draft change matching a reserved parity-validation family on live ADV state
then: The synthetic draft is not persisted to the live project state
The caller receives a clear error or bounded degraded outcome directing synthetic activity to isolated temp/test storage

scenarios.title: Legitimate parity wording remains allowed
given: A normal user-driven change proposal uses benign wording that mentions parity but does not match a reserved synthetic family
then: The draft change is persisted normally
The protection does not block or rename the legitimate draft

scenarios.title: Draft and status surfaces stay clear after validation activity
given: Supported internal validation activity has run
scenarios.when: adv_change_list with status draft or adv_status is executed on the live project
then: Stale synthetic parity-validation drafts are absent from live draft results
Real user-authored drafts remain visible

requirements.title: Status Config Diagnostics and Feature Flags
requirements.body: adv_status must surface project.json diagnostics and include parsed feature flag values so agents can see config health and runtime policy settings without opening files.

scenarios.title: Invalid project config is surfaced
given: project.json is malformed or schema-invalid
then: Output includes a config error or warning recommendation
The command does not fail hard due to config parse issues

scenarios.title: Feature flags are visible in status output
given: project.json parses successfully
then: Output includes feature_flags values
Defaults are applied when flags are omitted

requirements.title: OpenCode Session Debt Diagnostics
requirements.body: ADV diagnostics must safely detect stale blank assistant messages in the shared OpenCode session database, distinguish live in-flight rows from repairable stale debt, and require dry-run plus backup before any repair deletes rows.

scenarios.title: Status reports stale blank assistant debt read-only
given: The OpenCode database contains assistant messages with finish null and zero parts older than the stale threshold
scenarios.when: adv_status or an ADV doctor diagnostic is executed
then: The diagnostic opens the OpenCode database read-only
The output reports the count and bounded samples of stale blank assistant messages
A doctor recommendation is surfaced without modifying the database

scenarios.title: Live in-flight blank rows are excluded from repairable debt
given: The OpenCode database contains assistant messages with finish null and zero parts younger than the stale threshold
then: Younger rows are classified as live or in-flight
Younger rows are not counted as repairable stale debt
No repair recommendation is emitted solely because of younger rows

scenarios.title: Repair requires dry-run and backup
given: A repair utility is invoked against the OpenCode database
then: The utility refuses deletion unless apply mode is explicit
The utility refuses deletion unless a backup destination is provided and populated before deletion
Only assistant messages with finish null, zero parts, and age at or above the stale threshold are deleted

scenarios.title: Unavailable database degrades safely
given: The OpenCode database is missing, inaccessible, or the SQLite runtime is unavailable
then: The diagnostic returns an unavailable/degraded result
adv_status continues to complete
No destructive operation is attempted

requirements.title: Task Metadata Filter Semantics
requirements.body: Tasks may include optional metadata key/value pairs. adv_task_list must support has_metadata_key:<key> and metadata:<key>=<value> filters with behavior aligned between workflow-owned source-of-truth state and any derived query or index surface.

scenarios.title: Filter by metadata key
given: A change with tasks containing metadata keys
scenarios.when: adv_task_list is called with filter has_metadata_key:<key>
then: Only tasks containing that metadata key are returned

scenarios.title: Filter by metadata key/value
given: A change with tasks containing metadata key/value pairs
scenarios.when: adv_task_list is called with filter metadata:<key>=<value>
then: Only tasks matching both key and value are returned

requirements.title: Bounded Signal Flush on Shutdown
requirements.body: On SIGINT/SIGTERM, the plugin must run a bounded flush path before close, with idempotent/reentrant handling so duplicate signals cannot trigger multiple concurrent flush sequences.

scenarios.title: Signal performs bounded flush
given: The process receives SIGINT or SIGTERM
then: store.flush is attempted before store.close
A hard timeout bounds flush duration

scenarios.title: Duplicate signals are idempotent
given: A shutdown flush is already in progress
then: No second flush path starts
Shutdown remains deterministic

requirements.title: Source-Appropriate Due Diligence for Unknown Capability Questions
requirements.body: ADV-managed guidance (orchestrator agent text, synced overlays, and accompanying drift tests) must require source-appropriate due diligence before answering, recommending, or deciding on unknown platform, architecture, or capability questions. Diligence may use any appropriate mix of evidence sources (local code via lgrep/read, repo history or repo examples, GitHub examples, official docs, web research, or similar); the evidence bar is not a fixed source stack. Requests like "quick answer", "from your knowledge", or "don't research" may change response brevity only and must not lower the evidence bar. If required diligence cannot be completed, the response must stop and surface the blockage instead of presenting an unverified recommendation as settled.

scenarios.title: Unknown capability question triggers source-appropriate diligence
given: An unknown platform, architecture, or capability question is posed to an ADV-managed agent
scenarios.when: The agent prepares an answer, recommendation, or decision
then: The agent gathers source-appropriate evidence before answering
Evidence may come from local code inspection, repo history or repo examples, GitHub examples, official docs, web research, or other relevant sources chosen to fit the question
No carve-out permits skipping diligence on the basis that the question is local, short, or familiar

scenarios.title: Quick-answer requests change brevity only
given: The user requests a "quick answer", asks "from your knowledge", or says "don't research"
The question still requires due diligence under rq-dueDiligence01.1
then: The response may be shortened or compressed
The evidence bar is not lowered; diligence is still performed before recommending or deciding

scenarios.title: Blocked diligence stops and surfaces the blockage
given: Required diligence cannot be completed (for example: docs, research tools, or evidence sources are unavailable)
scenarios.when: The agent would otherwise present a directional answer or recommendation
then: The agent stops instead of presenting an unverified direction as settled
The response surfaces the specific blockage or missing evidence
No carve-out permits proceeding with an unverified recommendation

scenarios.title: Guidance surfaces and drift tests encode the rule
given: The repo contains ADV orchestrator agent text, synced overlays, and routing asset tests
then: The ADV agent and plan agent sources describe due-diligence-first routing for unknown capability questions
The synced overlays (adv.overlay.md, plan.overlay.md) carry the same rule
Regression tests fail if the legacy carve-out-first wording returns

requirements.title: Code-Enforced Prose Deduplication
requirements.body: ADV instruction surfaces (ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md, docs/command-voice-standard.md, .opencode/agents/adv.md, .opencode/command/adv-*.md) MUST classify each section by enforcement class (fully-enforced, partially-enforced, inherently-prose) and apply the matching compression template defined in docs/command-voice-standard.md § Prose-Load Reduction Rules. Sections whose behavior is fully or partially enforced by code MUST NOT contain paragraph explanations duplicating the enforced behavior; they MUST use a pointer line + constraint table format.

scenarios.title: Fully-enforced section uses pointer + table
given: A section in an ADV instruction surface describes behavior that is fully enforced by code (drift test, runtime guard, schema validation, tool formatter, or runtime tool requiring approval params)
then: The section opens with a pointer line referencing the enforcing code path
The section contains a constraint table summarizing the rule
The section does NOT contain paragraph explanations duplicating the enforced behavior

scenarios.title: Partially-enforced section adds gap rationale
given: A section describes behavior that is partially enforced by code (some aspects machine-checked, others rely on agent behavior)
then: The section uses the fully-enforced template (pointer + constraint table)
The section additionally contains a single line marked 'Agent-side gap:' describing what the code does NOT enforce

requirements.title: Drift Test Coverage for Compressed Prose
requirements.body: plugin/src/manifest-doc-drift.test.ts MUST contain structural assertions that verify compressed sections in ADV instruction surfaces conform to the enforcement-class templates. Assertions MUST be structural (line caps per class, presence of code-path reference in pointer line) and MUST NOT assert specific wording.

scenarios.title: Drift test enforces line caps per class
given: manifest-doc-drift.test.ts is inspected
then: An assertion verifies fully-enforced sections do not exceed the documented line cap
An assertion verifies partially-enforced sections do not exceed the documented line cap
An assertion verifies inherently-prose template sections do not exceed the documented line cap

scenarios.title: Drift test enforces code-path reference
given: A section is classified fully-enforced or partially-enforced
scenarios.when: The drift test inspects the section
then: The pointer line MUST contain a backtick-wrapped code-path reference matching `.+\.(ts|md|json)`
The assertion is structural; no specific wording is required

requirements.title: Category Classification Inventory
requirements.body: When a change executes prose-load reduction work, an inventory document MUST be produced and committed during execution that records every section being reclassified, its enforcement class, target compression format, code reference (for fully/partially-enforced classes), and gap rationale (for partially-enforced class). The inventory is a working document during execution; after compression completes it is marked as a post-compression archive and is not maintained thereafter. Durable invariants live in this spec, not in the inventory document.

scenarios.title: Inventory captures classification rows
given: A change executes prose-load reduction work
scenarios.when: The inventory document is inspected after the inventory pass completes
then: Every reclassified section appears as a row
Each row records: surface, section, line count, class (full/partial/inherent), target format, code reference (for full/partial), gap rationale (for partial), status

scenarios.title: Inventory marked archive after compression
given: All compression passes for a prose-reduction change have completed
scenarios.when: The inventory document header is inspected
then: The header records POST-COMPRESSION ARCHIVE status
No maintenance owner is assigned to the inventory thereafter

requirements.title: Inherently-Prose Constraint Templates
requirements.body: Sections classified inherently-prose (agent-side judgment, narration, or domain context that cannot be structurally enforced) MUST use a structured template (table, checklist, or trigger/action grid) and MUST NOT use paragraph prose. The structured template is the canonical scannable form for inherently-prose categories.

scenarios.title: Inherently-prose section uses structured template
given: A section is classified inherently-prose
then: The section opens with a one-line purpose statement
The section content uses a table, checklist, or trigger/action grid
The section does NOT contain paragraph prose explaining the rule

scenarios.title: Inherently-prose template excludes mandatory pointer
given: A section is classified inherently-prose (no code mechanism to point to)
then: The section MAY omit a code-path reference
The structural template is the only required form

requirements.title: Context-Shed Delegation Heuristic for Routing Tables
requirements.body: Delegation routing tables in ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md and adv-apply.md MUST include step 4.5 (Context-Shed Test) between risk-signal check (step 4) and default fallback (step 5). The test is a 4-question AND-conjunctive heuristic: (1) orchestrator already made design/architectural decisions for this task, (2) task's HOW does not feed into a downstream task's decisions, (3) acceptance criteria are fully defined before delegation, (4) task is mechanical implementation of a decided plan. All four must pass for delegate_allowed. Gated by floor: ~5 files touched OR ~50 lines changed. Conservative bias: when uncertain, default to inline_required. Step 4.5 MUST NOT override step 1 (human delegation_hint) or step 4 (risk signals).

scenarios.title: Step 4.5 inserted between step 4 and step 5 in both routing tables
given: ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md contains the Delegation Routing table
adv-apply.md contains the Delegation Routing table
then: Both tables contain a step 4.5 row between step 4 (risk signals) and step 5 (default)
Step 4.5 result is delegate_allowed when all four questions pass AND floor is met
Step 4.5 result is inline_required when any question fails or floor is not met
Step 1 (delegation_hint) and step 4 (risk signals) are unchanged

scenarios.title: Floor prevents micro-task delegation
given: A task touches fewer than ~5 files AND fewer than ~50 lines
All four context-shed questions pass
then: The floor check fails
Result is inline_required regardless of question answers

scenarios.title: AND-conjunction requires all four questions
given: A task passes 3 of 4 context-shed questions and meets the floor
then: Result is inline_required
Conservative bias preserves orchestrator context for borderline tasks

scenarios.title: Step 4.5 does not override human hint or risk signals
given: A task has metadata.delegation_hint set to inline_required
The context-shed test passes for the task
then: Step 1 returns inline_required
Step 4.5 is never reached

requirements.title: Context-Shed Prose in Orchestrator Agent and Post-Delegation P23 Scan
requirements.body: The adv.md orchestrator agent's Context-Optimal Execution section MUST include context-shed delegation criteria as prose bullets (NOT a routing table). Wording must reference the 4-question AND test and floor threshold. Additionally, adv-apply.md Task Flow MUST include a post-delegation P23 campsite-rule diff-scan step that checks same-pattern local subsystem issues after a delegated task returns, applying small/safe/local fixes inline and documenting scope-expanding findings as follow-ups without auto-fixing.

scenarios.title: adv.md contains context-shed prose bullets not table
given: The adv.md Context-Optimal Execution section is inspected
then: The section contains context-shed delegation criteria as prose bullets
The section does NOT contain a markdown routing table (no | pipe characters in table format)
The criteria reference the 4-question AND test and floor threshold

scenarios.title: adv-apply.md contains post-delegation P23 diff-scan step
given: The adv-apply.md Task Flow is inspected
then: A step after delegation spawn and before task completion performs a P23 campsite-rule diff-scan
The step diffs the sub-agent's touched files against pre-delegation baseline
Small/safe/local same-pattern fixes are applied inline
Scope-expanding findings are documented as follow-ups, not auto-fixed

scenarios.title: Drift tests enforce prose-only on adv.md and table on other surfaces
given: The drift test suite runs
then: ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md and adv-apply.md delegation tables contain step 4.5 with matching wording
adv.md Context-Optimal Execution section contains context-shed tokens without table pipe characters
adv-apply.md contains P23 diff-scan step tokens

requirements.title: adv_archive_purge tool
requirements.body: ADV must provide an explicit user-side lever to terminate an archived child workflow and remove its entry from the parent project workflow's in-memory change_summaries registry. The on-disk archive bundle is preserved by default; the destructive disk-removal escalation requires opt-in. After a workflow-only purge, adv_change_show for the purged change continues returning content from the on-disk projection.

scenarios.title: Workflow-only purge by default preserves disk bundle
given: An archived change with both an entry in the parent's change_summaries and an existing archive/<id>/change.json bundle on disk
scenarios.when: adv_archive_purge changeId: <id> is invoked without includeDiskBundle
then: The child workflow is terminated via Temporal client
The change is removed from the parent's change_summaries and source_versions
The on-disk archive bundle is preserved
adv_change_show for the changeId returns content from the on-disk projection

scenarios.title: Opt-in includeDiskBundle removes both workflow state and disk artifacts
given: An archived change with both workflow-state entry and disk bundle
scenarios.when: adv_archive_purge changeId: <id> includeDiskBundle: true is invoked
then: The child workflow is terminated and removed from change_summaries
The archive/<id>/ directory is recursively removed from disk
Subsequent adv_change_show returns the existing not-found error path

scenarios.title: Refuses non-archived or unknown changes
given: A change in active status, OR a changeId that does not exist in change_summaries
then: The tool returns a structured error and makes no state mutations

requirements.title: Bounded change_summaries registry
requirements.body: The parent project workflow's change_summaries registry MUST be bounded by a configurable cap. Default cap is 50 entries; the ADV_CHANGE_SUMMARIES_CAP environment variable overrides at process start. When a new archive summary signal would push the registry past the cap, the oldest archived entry (by lastActivityAt) is evicted. Active and other non-archived statuses are never evicted regardless of count. Eviction is replay-deterministic.

scenarios.title: Insertion at cap does not evict
given: A project workflow whose change_summaries holds exactly N entries (where N equals the configured cap)
scenarios.when: applyChangeSummaryToProjectState is called with an existing changeId (in-place update)
then: The registry size remains exactly N
No entry is evicted

scenarios.title: Insertion past cap evicts oldest archived entry
given: A project workflow whose change_summaries already holds N entries (one archived, the rest in mixed statuses)
scenarios.when: applyChangeSummaryToProjectState is called with a new (N+1)th changeId
then: The oldest entry with status archived (by lastActivityAt) is removed from change_summaries and source_versions
Active and non-archived entries are preserved

scenarios.title: Eviction is replay-deterministic
given: A workflow history that triggered eviction during a prior run
then: The same entry is evicted with the same final state
Replay-determinism guards in the test suite pass

requirements.title: Per-tool safety-net timeout overrides
requirements.body: The plugin's safety-net wrapper has a default 10s timeout (DEFAULT_TOOL_TIMEOUT_MS in safe-execute.ts). Tools whose execute body legitimately exceeds 10s on a mature project MUST declare an explicit timeoutMs override at registration time, with a code comment citing the inner-budget rationale. The default value remains 10s; raising the global default is not permitted.

scenarios.title: Long-running tools declare an explicit override
given: A tool whose execute body wraps a subprocess or workflow operation that legitimately exceeds 10s
scenarios.when: The tool is registered in tool-registry.ts
then: The registration uses safeExecute with an explicit { timeoutMs: N } where N is sufficient for the inner budget plus modest headroom
A code comment cites the inner-budget rationale and references this requirement

scenarios.title: adv_temporal_worker_restart returns within 1s on success via fire-and-forget
given: A worker restart that takes longer than 10s to fully complete
then: The tool returns within 1s with success:true and a verification hint pointing at adv_status / adv_temporal_diagnose
The underlying restart proceeds asynchronously
An async failure is logged via appendDebugLog without surfacing a ToolExecutionTimeout

requirements.title: adv_change_bulk_close composes disk sweep
requirements.body: After a successful adv_change_bulk_close, both workflow state and on-disk source artifacts (changes/<id>/change.json, proposal.md) MUST be removed in the same call for changes whose individual close succeeded. Per-id outcomes are reported in diskRemoved and diskFailed arrays in the response. Mid-flight workflow-close failure preserves source dirs as the orphan-sweep recovery path.

scenarios.title: Successful bulk-close removes disk artifacts and reports per-id results
given: Multiple draft changes selected for closure with explicit user approval
scenarios.when: adv_change_bulk_close is invoked and the underlying closeBatch succeeds
then: Each closed change's source directory is removed via sweepClosedChangesFromDisk
The response includes diskRemoved and diskFailed arrays per changeId
Idempotency guarantees of the helper apply (already-missing dirs are reported as removed)

scenarios.title: Partial workflow-close failure preserves source dirs
given: A bulk-close where the overall closeBatch reports success:false (one or more closures failed)
then: Source dirs for failed closures are NOT removed
The orphan sweeper (adv_archive_sweep_orphans includeClosed: true) remains the documented recovery path

requirements.title: Test-mode synthetic project_id guardrail
requirements.body: During vitest runs (process.env.VITEST === 'true' or process.env.ADV_TEST_MODE === '1'), getProjectId MUST NOT resolve to a real git root commit SHA from a fixture path. For a real-git directory it returns a path-derived synthetic ID with a recognizable prefix; for a non-git fixture it returns null (preserving the legacy in-repo path fallback). This prevents test fixtures from leaking state into a real ADV project's external state directory.

scenarios.title: Vitest run resolves to a synthetic ID with the SYNTHETIC_TEST_PROJECT_ID_PREFIX
given: process.env.VITEST is 'true' and the directory is a real git repo
then: The returned ID is 40 hex chars
The ID starts with SYNTHETIC_TEST_PROJECT_ID_PREFIX (16 leading zeros)
Distinct directories produce distinct synthetic IDs (cross-project test isolation)

scenarios.title: Vitest run on a non-git directory returns null
given: process.env.VITEST is 'true' and the directory is not a git repo (e.g. a createTestProject fixture with a stub .git and no commits)
then: The function returns null
Callers fall back to legacy in-repo paths via their existing 'targetProjectId ? getExternalRoot(...) : undefined' patterns

scenarios.title: Hard-fail guardrail asserts override is active during test runs
given: The vitest test suite runs in the plugin checkout
then: process.env.VITEST is 'true'
getProjectId(process.cwd()) returns a synthetic ID, not the real root commit SHA
Resolving a real git SHA from this code path is a hard test failure

requirements.title: Singleton Temporal worker per project across sessions
requirements.body: When multiple plugin instances initialize against the same external state directory for the same project, at most ONE Temporal worker process MUST exist for that project_id at any given time. A file-lock sentinel at {external-state-dir}/{project-id}/worker.lock coordinates ownership. Subsequent instances participate as Temporal clients only. Stale-PID detection (process.kill(pid, 0)) reclaims the lock when the recorded owner has exited.

scenarios.title: First plugin instance acquires lock and spawns worker
given: No worker.lock file exists in the project external state directory
then: A worker.lock file is created atomically (O_CREAT | O_EXCL) with the plugin process PID
An out-of-process Temporal worker is spawned via the existing out-of-process-worker.ts path
Cleanup hooks (process exit / SIGINT / SIGTERM) release the lock and terminate the worker

scenarios.title: Subsequent plugin instance reads lock and skips worker spawn
given: A worker.lock file exists and the recorded PID is alive (process.kill(pid, 0) succeeds or throws EPERM)
scenarios.when: A second plugin initializes against the same project
then: The acquireWorkerLock helper reports owned:false with ownerPid
No additional worker process is spawned
The plugin still initializes the Temporal client and participates as a client only

scenarios.title: Stale lock from dead PID is reclaimed
given: A worker.lock file exists but the recorded PID is no longer alive (process.kill(pid, 0) throws ESRCH)
scenarios.when: A plugin attempts to acquire the lock
then: The stale lock file is removed
Acquisition is retried once
On success the new plugin owns the lock and spawns its own worker

scenarios.title: ADV_FORCE_IN_PROCESS_WORKER bypasses singleton lock
given: process.env.ADV_FORCE_IN_PROCESS_WORKER === '1'
then: The lock-acquisition step is skipped
The legacy in-process worker path is used
This rollback path supports per-session debugging when needed
